field_decisions.reason: The product context is Luxembourg-specific, but the core legal frame is EU Article 4 AI literacy.

field_decisions.value: Article 4 AI literacy duties already apply from 2 February 2025 to providers and deployers of AI systems, while the CSSF/BCL thematic review shows why AI governance, explainability, ethics, bias, fairness, and auditability matter in Luxembourg finance. The reviewed EU guidance does not impose a finance-specific template or governance board.
field_decisions.reason: This is the narrowest defensible regulatory synthesis from the official sources.
Public Luxembourg finance-sector AI courses are clearly available from House of Training.

field_decisions.value: Luxembourg company-training co-funding covers external training providers and offers 15% support, with a 35% wage-cost uplift for qualifying participants.
field_decisions.reason: This is directly stated on the Luxembourg government page and does not depend on product-specific interpretation.

value: Product-specific Fit 4 AI applicability is unverified
No public CSSF, CNPD, or accreditation route was found for A4 itself
The 461+ Luxembourg financial-institutions count is unverified
The 20-30% operational cost-reduction claim is unverified
AML/KYC automation and audit-documentation deliverables remain product-design claims
field_decisions.reason: These are the main fail-closed reasons that keep the product in review.

field_decisions.value: Luxembourg banks, funds, PSFs, insurers, and adjacent compliance, risk, and operations teams using or evaluating AI.
field_decisions.omission_reason: Useful positioning context, but still partly inferential and not a default dashboard fact.

field_decisions.value: Fit 4 AI is a real Luxembourg SME assessment-and-roadmap route with co-financing, but the reviewed page does not prove that A4 itself is eligible or that any 30% training rate applies.
field_decisions.omission_reason: The public route exists, but product-level fit remains unverified.
Training-only versus training-plus-consulting remains a product-design decision, not a public fact.
The public support routes exist, but the reviewed sources do not prove that A4 itself qualifies.
No public CSSF, CNPD, or other accreditation / endorsement route was found for the product itself.
The 461+ Luxembourg financial-institutions figure was not re-verified with a primary source.
The 20-30% operational cost-reduction claim has no public evidence in this rerun.
